phoneme_inventory.vowel_count.note: "the five basic Japanese vowel sounds (あ, い, う, え and お)" — syntax.txt

phoneme_inventory.consonant_gemination_c.note: small っ vs つ pairs: はっか 'ignition' vs はつか '20 days'; しっけ vs しつけ — syntax.txt

phoneme_inventory.moraic_nasal_is_separa.note: "キャンプ is three beats long: spelled out, it will be pronounced きゃ, ん and ぷ" — syntax.txt

mora_timing.timing_unit.note: "the pronunciation of Japanese as mora ... each basic syllable is one beat long" — syntax.txt

mora_timing.long_vowel_extra_unit.note: "a long vowel in Japanese is a combination of two vowels, pronounced over two 'drum beats'" — syntax.txt

mora_timing.n_counts_as_unit.note: "キャンプ is three beats long ... きゃ, ん and ぷ" — ん counts as its own beat — syntax.txt

rendaku.applies_to.note: "片(かた) + 付(つ)く → 片付(かたづ)く and 鼻(はな) + 血(ち) → 鼻血(はなぢ)" — syntax.txt

rendaku.change_direction.note: "in compound words in which voicing occurs": つ→づ, ち→ぢ (voiceless kana gains voicing) — syntax.txt

rendaku.fully_predictable.note: "there are no rules to tell when something will, or will not voice" — syntax.txt

kana_orthography.particle_topic_spelled.note: "は (pronounced わ) is used to disambiguate statements" — particles.txt

kana_orthography.particle_direction_spel.note: "the particle へ is always pronounced え" — particles.txt

kana_orthography.particle_object_spelled.note: "the particle [を] is always pronounced as お" — particles.txt

kana_orthography.sokuon_symbol.note: "the doubling is represented by ... a つ (or ツ) written either half height ... or half width" — syntax.txt

kana_orthography.katakana_long_vowel_mar.note: "In katakana, long vowels are really just that, a vowel with a dash" (アー, イー...) — syntax.txt

loanword_katakana.script_for_loanwords.note: "katakana is used ... for words that have been imported into Japanese from other languages" — syntax.txt

loanword_katakana.v_sound_kana.note: v(1) ヴァ/ヴェ/ヴィ/ヴォ/ヴ; "v (2) uses the ば—column. Preferred to 'v (1)'" — syntax.txt

loanword_katakana.transliteration_fully_.note: "loan words may have a different written form than expected": ブリュッセル, イギリス — syntax.txt

punctuation_layout.words_separated_by_sp.note: kanji "act as word boundary indicators"; "hard to tell without spaces" — syntax.txt, verb_grammar.txt

punctuation_layout.question_mark_require.note: "The symbol ? is written the same way as in English, but typically the particle か is used instead" — syntax.txt

word_order.basic.note: "Japanese is an SOV language ... most sentences are of the form 'we, something do'" — syntax.txt

word_order.adposition_type.note: particles suffixed after the word: 犬が, ご飯を; "whatever は is suffixed to" — syntax.txt, particles.txt

word_order.modifier_position.note: attributives precede: "きれいだ → きれいな[noun]", コーヒーを飲む人 — more_verb_grammar.txt

word_order.verb_strictly_final_in_neutra.note: SOV: "if there is a verb, it will generally come last" — glossary.txt

conjugation.regular_verb_classes.note: "These regular verbs fall into two categories, namely ... 五段 ... and ... 一段" — syntax.txt

conjugation.irregular_verbs.note: "There are three irregular verbs in Japanese ... する ... 来(く)る ... ある" — verb_grammar.txt

conjugation.i_adjectives_inflect.note: "Verbal adjectives — the class of adjectives in Japanese that inflect just like regular verbs do" — verb_grammar.txt

conjugation.na_adjectives_need_copula.note: "we must change だ from its 終止形 ... to 連体形": きれいだ → きれいな[noun] — more_verb_grammar.txt

conjugation.person_number_agreement.note: one form for all persons: 行きます = "(I, you, he, she, it, we, they)'ll go" — particles.txt

counters.numeral_needs_counter.note: "To count in Japanese, two things are required: a number, and a categorical marker" — counters.txt

counters.counter_people.note: "人(にん・り) — People ... For normal counting of people, stick with 人" — counters.txt

counters.generic_native_counter.note: "counting things: つ" (一つ...十), counters "used for general counting of items" — counters.txt

counters.sound_changes_occur.note: "they may contract, and the counter may become voiced": いち + はい becomes いっぱい — counters.txt

honorifics_keigo.main_categories.note: 丁寧語 politeness; "humble (謙譲) and honorific (尊敬) speech patterns" — more_verb_grammar.txt

honorifics_keigo.polite_verb_suffix.note: "Politeness, or the use of 丁寧語 ... revolves around using です and ます" (ます on verbs) — more_verb_grammar.txt

honorifics_keigo.honorific_prefix_exists.note: "The honorific prefix": お for 訓読み words, ご for 音読み words — particles.txt

honorifics_keigo.sonkeigo_refers_to.note: "things pertaining to the person of higher social status are elevated by using honorific patterns" — more_verb_grammar

honorifics_keigo.kenjougo_refers_to.note: "Humble speech applies to everything that has to do with oneself" — more_verb_grammar.txt

sentence_final_particles.ka_function.note: "か — Questioning particle ... acts as the question mark when used at the end of sentences" — particles.txt

sentence_final_particles.ne_function.note: "placed at the end of a sentence, when the speaker wants to provoke the listener into agreeing" — particles.txt

sentence_final_particles.yo_function.note: "marks information as being either new information, or contrary to the listener's belief" — particles.txt

sentence_final_particles.gender_associat.note: わ "considered an effeminate particle ... used a lot by women, but avoided by men"; ぜ/ぞ informal — particles.txt

forms_of_address.neutral_polite_suffix.note: さん: "the standard name suffix that is used across Japan" — language_patterns.txt

forms_of_address.second_person_pronoun_c.note: "use personal pronouns as little as possible"; あなた "impolite outside of very formal settings" — language_patterns.txt

forms_of_address.teacher_title.note: "a doctor is a 先生, as is a school teacher, a lawyer" — language_patterns.txt
